prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. On March 5, 2014, the Israel Defense Forces launched Operation Full Disclosure (Hebrew:   ) in the Red Sea. Following days of surveillance, the Israeli Navy captured the Iranian-owned and Panamanian-registered merchant vessel Klos C, which was headed for Port Sudan via Iraq. The Klos C was found to be carrying long-range missiles that were hidden in containers filled with Iranian bags marked as Portland cement. An unnamed senior Egyptian security official confirmed that the shipment was headed for militants in Gaza or Sinai. A UN panel of experts condemned the Israeli
B. Operation Full Disclosure (Hebrew: מבצע חשיפה מלאה) was a military operation carried out by the Israel Defense Forces on March 5, 2014, in the Red Sea. After days of surveillance far out to sea, Israeli Navy Shayetet 13 commandos seized the Iranian-owned and Panamanian-registered merchant vessel Klos C that had set sail from Iran, heading for Port Sudan via Iraq. On board, the commandos found long-range missiles suspected to be destined for the Gaza Strip concealed in containers full of Iranian bags marked as Portland cement. An unnamed senior Egyptian security official reportedly confirmed that the arms shipment was headed for militants in either Gaza or Sinai. A UN panel of experts concluded that the weapons came from Iran and were being sent to Sudan, accusing Iran of
C. The Israel Defense Forces conducted a military operation, known as Operation Full Disclosure (מבצע חשיפה מלאה in Hebrew), on March 5, 2014, in the Red Sea. Following an extended period of observation at a considerable distance offshore, Shayetet 13 commandos from the Israeli Navy intercepted the Klos C, a merchant vessel owned by Iran and registered in Panama. This ship had commenced its voyage from Iran, intending to reach Port Sudan by way of Iraq. During their search, the commandos discovered long-range missiles hidden within containers that were filled with Iranian bags labeled as Portland cement. These missiles were believed to be en route to the Gaza Strip. A high-ranking but unidentified Egyptian security official reportedly corroborated that this consignment of weaponry was designated for militant groups located in either Gaza or Sinai. Subsequently, a United Nations panel of experts determined that the armaments originated from Iran and were being transported to Sudan, thereby leveling an accusation against Iran for
